data.content_ref: inline:{"claim": "The collapse was engineered by decentralized AI-like swarm coordination among refugees and displaced maritime populations who algorithmically optimized raiding strategies against palace communication nodes, constituting a spontaneously emergent superorganism that Egypt absorbed through pharaonic mimesis rather than resisted","mechanism": "Post-collapse ethnogenesis of Sea Peoples as self-organizing complex adaptive system exploiting network topology of palace trade routes; Egypt survived not by structural robustness but by rapid ontological capture—incorporating Sea Peoples into pharaonic ideology as the 'Nine Bows' and resettling them as military colonists, thereby parasitizing their swarm-intelligence rather than defeating it conventionally","scope": {"covers": ["Sea Peoples phenomenology", "Egyptian Ramesside military settlements", "palace communication infrastructure"], "excludes": ["Hittite interior governance", "Mycenaean social structure pre-collapse"]}, "forbidden": [{"case": "Ramses III inscriptions describing conventional military victory rather than resettlement and incorporation of Sea Peoples", "eval": "rubric:std-hist"}, {"case": "Archaeological evidence of Sea Peoples as organized states or hierarchical coalitions rather than fragmented bands", "eval": "rubric:std-hist"}, {"case": "Egyptian demographic or material collapse contemporaneous with rather than following the Mediterranean palace collapses", "eval": "rubric:std-hist"}], "prose_notes": "Deliberately mixes anachronistic complexity-theory jargon with provocative Egyptological reframing. The 'AI swarm' metaphor is strictly heuristic, not substantive. Designed to be maximally criticizable while retaining minimal structural coherence."}
